title: Event payload: policy_eval (v0.1)
description: Payload shape for policy_eval events. Records the evaluation of a specific policy version against a subject, with the resulting decision and optional reasoning. References a policy version via 'policyVersionRef', which MUST match the 'policyVersionId' of some policy.published event (in this Pack or resolvable elsewhere).

policyEvalId.description: Unique identifier for this specific evaluation. Used when other events reference this evaluation in their envelope causality.parents or contextEvents (e.g. a tool_result with outcome 'denied' referencing the policy_eval that produced the denial).

runId.description: The run this evaluation belongs to. MAY be omitted for project-level evaluations not associated with a single run.

policyRef.description: Stable identifier for the policy across versions. SHOULD correspond to the 'policyId' in one or more policy.published events.

policyVersionRef.description: Identifier of the specific policy version applied in this evaluation. MUST match the 'policyVersionId' of the policy.published event that established this version. Free-form string — implementers may use semver, git hashes, URIs, or sequential integers, consistent across their own events.

decision.description: The outcome of the evaluation. Canonical values: 'allow', 'deny', 'require_review', 'error'. Enumeration is OPEN — implementers MAY use other values, and validators MUST NOT reject solely on unknown values (per ADR 0005).

subject.description: What was evaluated. Open shape. Typical fields: 'type' (e.g. 'tool_call', 'action', 'input', 'registration'), 'ref' (an eventId or other reference; SHOULD also appear in the envelope's contextEvents when it is an eventId in this Pack), 'description'. SHOULD be present unless the policy applies to ambient context with no specific subject.

inputs.description: Data the policy considered (attributes, request body, context). Open shape. Distinct from 'subject': subject identifies what is being judged, inputs are the data the policy actually looked at to make its decision.

reasoning.description: Explanation of the decision. Free text or structured (rule trace, satisfied/unsatisfied predicates, etc.). Open shape.
